questions.question: Which of the following is a function of framing in link control?
options: A] To identify the source and destination of data
B] To control the flow of data
C] To detect and correct errors
questions.explanation: Framing is used to identify the source and destination of data, making it easier to route and manage data packets.

questions.question: Which data link layer protocol is an example of connection-oriented protocols?
questions.explanation: TCP (Transmission Control Protocol) is a connection-oriented protocol, which ensures reliable data transfer by maintaining a connection between the sender and receiver.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of flow control in link control?
options: A] To detect and correct errors
B] To control the amount of data sent at one time
questions.explanation: Flow control is used to regulate the amount of data sent at one time, preventing network congestion and ensuring efficient data transfer.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a connectionless data link layer protocol?
questions.explanation: UDP (User Datagram Protocol) is a connectionless protocol, which does not establish a connection before sending data, and does not guarantee delivery or order of packets.

questions.question: What is the main function of error control in link control?
questions.explanation: Error control is used to detect and correct errors that occur during data transmission, ensuring data integrity and reliability.
